title: Calceolaria radiculoides
taxonomy: Calceolaria radiculoides is a member of the family Scrophulariaceae. This classification places it within the order Lamiales, which comprises a diverse group of flowering plants. The family Scrophulariaceae is a large and complex group that includes several genera related to Calceolaria radiculoides. The genus Calceolaria itself is native to the Andes mountains in South America. Calceolaria radiculoides is also known to be part of the subfamily Calceolarioideae within the Scrophulariaceae family.

intro: Calceolaria radiculoides, commonly known as the "pocket flower", is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the Andes region of South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Calceolaria radiculoides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Calceolaria radiculoides is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are traditionally used to treat skin conditions, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
